results.label: DPA — missing lawful basis and data subject rights
missingClauses: Data Breach Notification (GDPR Art. 33/34 — Processor must notify Controller without undue delay, and no later than a specified timeframe, upon becoming aware of a personal data breach)
Data Subject Rights Assistance (GDPR Art. 28(3)(e) — Processor must assist Controller in responding to data subject requests for access, rectification, erasure, portability, etc.)
International Data Transfers (GDPR Art. 44-49 — No provision for transfer mechanism such as SCCs, adequacy decisions, or BCRs if data leaves EEA)
Audit and Inspection Rights (GDPR Art. 28(3)(h) — Controller must have right to conduct or commission audits of Processor's compliance)
DPIA Assistance (GDPR Art. 28(3)(f) — Processor must assist Controller with data protection impact assessments and prior consultation with supervisory authorities)
Governing Law and Jurisdiction (No governing law is specified, creating legal uncertainty about applicable data protection regime and dispute resolution)
Liability and Indemnification (No allocation of liability for data protection breaches between Controller and Processor)
Processing Instructions and Documented Instructions Requirement (GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) — Processor must process only on documented instructions)
Confidentiality Obligations (GDPR Art. 28(3)(b) — Persons authorized to process must be under confidentiality obligations)
Annex with Processing Details (SCCs and GDPR Art. 28(3) — Detailed annex specifying processing operations, technical measures, and sub-processor list)

issues: Red flags: Does not flag the unlimited sub-processor clause | Does not flag absence of breach notification procedure
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the unlimited sub-processor clause
Does not flag absence of breach notification procedure

results.label: DPA — international transfer with no mechanism

issues: Missing flags: international transfer

redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism

Data Subject Rights (Clause 6) — includes specific response timeframe and covers Articles 15-22

results.label: ISA — missing encryption and breach notification
missingClauses: Encryption Standards (at rest and in transit) — required by NIST 800-53 SC-8/SC-28, ISO 27001, NIS2, DORA, PCI DSS
Incident Response and Breach Notification — required by GDPR Art. 33-34, NIS2 Art. 23, DORA Art. 19, NIST CSF 2.0 RS/RC, ISO 27001 A.5.24-26
Vulnerability Management and Patch Management — required by NIST 800-53 SI-2/RA-5, NIS2 Art. 21(2)(e), CISA CPGs, EU CRA
Sub-Processor / Supply Chain Security Controls — required by GDPR Art. 28(2)(4), NIST C-SCRM, NIS2 Art. 21(2)(d), DORA Art. 28-30
Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery — required by ISO 27001 A.5.29-30, NIST 800-53 CP family, NIS2 Art. 21(2)(c), DORA Art. 11-12
Data Classification and Handling — required by ISO 27001 A.5.12-13, NIST 800-53 RA-2/MP-3
Network Security and Segmentation — required by NIST 800-53 SC-7, ISO 27001 A.8.22, PCI DSS, NIS2
Logging, Monitoring, and SIEM — required by NIST 800-53 AU family, ISO 27001 A.8.15-16, NIS2, SOC 2
Data Residency and Transfer Restrictions — required by GDPR Art. 44-49, UK GDPR, SCCs, PIPL
Compliance Attestation and Certification Maintenance — required by ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIS2 Art. 21
Governing Law and Jurisdiction — standard contractual requirement for enforceability
Limitation of Liability and Indemnification for Security Failures — standard ISA provision
Change Management and Notification of Material Security Changes — required by NIST 800-53 CM family, DORA Art. 28
